{"action":"created","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy/issues/27821","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy/issues/27821/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy/issues/27821/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy/issues/27821/events","html_url":"https://github.com/numpy/numpy/issues/27821","id":2685218494,"node_id":"I_kwDOAA3dP86gDS6-","number":27821,"title":"ENH: Compute Jacobian matrices","user":{"login":"b-hedden","id":94723403,"node_id":"U_kgDOBaVdSw","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/94723403?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/b-hedden","html_url":"https://github.com/b-hedden","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/b-hedden/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/b-hedden/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/b-hedden/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/b-hedden/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/b-hedden/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/b-hedden/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/b-hedden/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/b-hedden/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/b-hedden/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":1,"created_at":"2024-11-23T03:17:40Z","updated_at":"2024-11-23T19:58:34Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"NONE","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"### Proposed new feature or change:

NumPy currently lacks a built-in function for calculating Jacobian matrices. Although it is possible to find a Jacobian matrix by applying `np.gradient` to each component separately, or by using finite differences manually. a built-in function such as `np.jacobian` would improve usability. It seems that this addition would fit within the scope of NumPy. This could become quite complicated, as the user would need to define some function f(x) themselves for computing the Jacobian, but this feels like a worthwhile feature for NumPy, as other similar python libraries have built-in support for this.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy/issues/27821/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy/issues/27821/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy/issues/comments/2495637519","html_url":"https://github.com/numpy/numpy/issues/27821#issuecomment-2495637519","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy/issues/27821","id":2495637519,"node_id":"IC_kwDOAA3dP86UwGgP","user":{"login":"izzzabellay","id":150369264,"node_id":"U_kgDOCPZz8A","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/150369264?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/izzzabellay","html_url":"https://github.com/izzzabellay","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/izzzabellay/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/izzzabellay/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/izzzabellay/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/izzzabellay/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/izzzabellay/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/izzzabellay/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/izzzabellay/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/izzzabellay/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/izzzabellay/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2024-11-23T19:58:33Z","updated_at":"2024-11-23T19:58:33Z","author_association":"NONE","body":"This sounds like a good idea. 
If no one else is working on it right now, I can try to develop a built-in function for Jacobian matrix calculation and will make a pull request before Dec.10, as a part of my school assignment.

03:02:47Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"NONE","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"Hello guys!
I am running some codes on a jupiter notebook remote server. As I can´t install graphviz, I am trying to use ipycytoscape to renderize the visualize results.

If I run a example code, it does create an html file containing the visualization

```
import dask
import dask.array as da

with dask.config.set({"visualization.engine": "cytoscape"}):
    x = da.ones((15, 15), chunks=(5, 5))
    y = x + x.T
    y.visualize()
```
![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/f3d2f61c-87e3-4670-bdeb-f09fdfaed658)

So far, no issue.

But as soon as I tries do display my real dask.dataframe graphs, it ignores the parameters and tries to use graphviz
import dask

```
df = dask.dataframe.read_parquet(path= 'Temp/', dtype_backend='pyarrow')
with dask.config.set({"visualization.engine": "cytoscape"}):
    df.visualize()
```

![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/ce7cb574-46a7-429e-b3c7-aced63a8f5a1)


```
---------------------------------------------------------------------------
```

- Python version: 3.11.9
- Install method (conda, pip, source): PIP
